what_it_does: This file is a structured extraction and design-system audit of an 18-page investor/partner-facing company-info deck for ConceptV, recording the narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and a catalog of visual patterns with recipes, evidence slide references, and prescriptive generator rules.
observations: The deck is analyzed slide-by-slide via an attention_flow array that describes where the eye enters and moves on each page.

Findings are grouped into categories such as gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, sequence, and emergent-content-budget.
Every finding includes a recipe, evidence slide IDs, an emergent boolean, and a generator_rule.
The palette is tightly constrained: warm off-white background (#F8F6F2), a single golden accent (#B8960C), charcoal text, and no dark mode.
Shape semantics are strict: hexagons for system/product names, circles for entities/connections, arrows for direction/action.
The cover and closer slides are full-bleed warm architectural photos that mirror each other, bracketing the argument.
Before/after network diagrams appear as a repeated transformation pattern.
A small V-mark logo icon is required in the bottom-right corner of every interior slide.
An architectural map watermark texture is applied to all interior slides at very low contrast.
Strict content budgets are enforced: headings, labels, buttons, and captions must stay single-line with prescribed character ranges.
standouts: The emergent-content-budget rule that treats any wrapped heading or label as a design error and prescribes exact character budgets.
The open/close photo mirror that creates an emotional bracket around the deck.
The before/after diagram pair used as the core visual argument for complexity-to-order transformation.
The strict shape semantics (hexagon/circle/arrow) and single golden accent that define a concise visual language.
conventions: Follows a consistent JSON schema with collection, depth_level, what_it_is, narrative_arc, attention_flow, and findings.
Each finding uses the same fields: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule.
Evidence is cited with slide IDs like p-01, p-02, etc.
Color and sizing values are given as approximate observed values with hex codes and pixel ranges.
Generator rules are written prescriptively using 'always'/'never' language derived from the observed deck.
concerns: The file is truncated at the end, cutting off the final finding in the emergent-content-budget category.
Color, font, and size values are described as approximate/observed rather than measured from source files.
The strict 'never'/'always' generator rules are inferred from a single 18-slide deck and may not generalize.
No source format, canvas dimensions, or measurement methodology are provided.
Character budgets assume a specific 1120x630 canvas scale.
notes: A dense, rule-heavy design autopsy of a ConceptV pitch deck that treats every visual choice as a generator constraint.
questions: What is the original source format and how were these values extracted?
Are the hex color values measured or estimated?
What are the actual canvas dimensions and font families?
Is the truncated final finding recoverable from another source?
Does this represent a final production deck or a design concept?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the deck's design system and narrative structure, which matches the file's actual content.
apparent_themes: Visual argument through ordered slide sequence.
Restraint and consistency in palette, texture, and iconography.
Content discipline with single-line labels and calibrated bullet lengths.
Warm, premium residential aesthetic anchored by golden-hour photography.
Before/after transformation as proof of value.
Semantic geometry where shapes encode meaning.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to encode the deck's visual and rhetorical language into reproducible generator rules, so future slides or variants can be produced in the same voice and structure.
novelty: The combination of strict content budgets that forbid wrapping, explicit shape semantics, and the structural open/close photo bracket is unusually systematic for a pitch-deck audit.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, rule-based design vocabulary for light-mode pitch decks, including warm palette values, shape semantics, content budgets, attention-flow descriptions, and per-slide pattern evidence that a fused system could reference when generating or auditing similar decks.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a structured design-system extraction of an 18-page ConceptV investor pitch deck, cataloguing narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, and visual patterns with generator rules, color values, and content budgets.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to capture the deck's visual and rhetorical conventions so they can be reproduced or audited in a fused design substrate.
